---
title: "Tutorial Frontmatter Template"
description: "Canonical YAML frontmatter and Colab badge convention for OpenPlains Learning tutorials. Copy the block below into new `.qmd` files."
author: "OpenPlains"
date: "2026-05-19"
date-modified: today
categories: [meta, template]
draft: true
---

This file is the **single source of truth** for the frontmatter shape that
notebook tutorials, blog posts, and workshop pages should use. It is marked
`draft: true` so it never appears on the public site.

## Copy this block into new tutorials

```yaml
---
title: "Sentence-case tutorial title"
description: "One-sentence summary used in listing cards and meta tags."
author: "Corey T. White"
date: "YYYY-MM-DD"                 # ISO 8601, zero-padded, quoted
date-modified: today
image: images/cover.png            # relative to this qmd file
categories: [tag-one, tag-two]     # kebab-case, flow-sequence array
draft: false                       # explicit; flip to true for WIP

page-layout: full
title-block-banner: true

format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 4
    code-copy: true
    code-fold: false
    code-tools: true
    lightbox: true

execute:
  enabled: true
  eval: true
  freeze: auto                     # set to true only for slow / heavy notebooks
  cache: true
  keep-ipynb: true                 # required when a Colab badge is used

jupyter: python3
---
```

## Colab badge convention

Immediately after the frontmatter, add a Colab badge pointing at the `.ipynb`
companion of the tutorial:

```markdown
[![Open in Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/assets/colab-badge.svg)](https://colab.research.google.com/github/OpenPlainsInc/openplainsinc.github.io/blob/main/<PATH_TO_IPYNB>)
```

Replace `<PATH_TO_IPYNB>` with the repo-relative path to the notebook
(e.g. `content/notebooks/stac_tutorial/t_stac_tutorial.ipynb`).

The badge only resolves if the notebook is **committed to the repo** — Colab
fetches it from GitHub, not from the rendered site. Generate it from the qmd
and commit it alongside:

```bash
quarto convert content/notebooks/<dir>/<name>.qmd   # writes <name>.ipynb
git add content/notebooks/<dir>/<name>.ipynb
```

Re-run the conversion and re-commit whenever the tutorial's code cells change.
The Code Quality workflow fails if a Colab badge points at a path that does not
exist in the repo.

## Field reference

| Field | Required | Notes |
|------|----------|-------|
| `title` | yes | Quoted; sentence case. |
| `description` | yes | One sentence; shows on listing cards. |
| `author` | yes | String (not list) for single-author. Use a YAML list only for multi-author. |
| `date` | yes | ISO 8601 (`"YYYY-MM-DD"`), zero-padded, quoted. |
| `date-modified` | no | `today` is fine; auto-updates on render. |
| `image` | yes | Cover image for listing cards; relative to the qmd. |
| `categories` | yes | Flow-sequence array, kebab-case tags. |
| `draft` | yes | Always explicit (`true` or `false`), never omitted. |
| `execute.keep-ipynb` | conditional | `true` keeps a locally rendered `.ipynb` for authors; the committed Colab notebook is generated with `quarto convert` (see above). |
| `execute.freeze` | no | `auto` by default; `true` only for expensive notebooks. |
